title: viburnum opulus subsp. americanum
intro: Viburnum opulus subsp. americanum, also known as the American cranberry bush, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its clusters of small white flowers and red or yellow berries. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Viburnum opulus subsp. americanum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
